title: Quamoclit pennata
intro: Quamoclit pennata, also known as the coral creeper or clematis, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia and Australia, characterized by its twining, woody vines and clusters of small, star-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Quamoclit pennata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Quamoclit pennata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is commonly used to make cough syrups, expectorants, and other respiratory remedies.

This family belongs to the order Solanales and the class Magnoliopsida. The genus Quamoclit is further classified under the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants, and the division Magnoliophyta, which includes flowering plants. The classification of Quamoclit pennata is a key aspect of its identification and understanding within the botanical community.
